rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE FULLER VS. SPECIALIZED LOAN SERVICING, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Partial Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 31, 2018, Line 2. DEFENDANTS SPECIALIZED LOAN SERVICING, LLC, DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, AS TRUSTEE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative, Partial Summary Adjudication. DENIED in part and Hearing Required in Part. Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Motion for Summary Adjudication as to negligence cause of action is DENIED. Moving party based its motion for summary adjudication on Plaintiff's failure to "sufficiently allege" or "plead" various elements of this cause of action. Sufficiency of pleadings has been previously addressed and overruled on demurrer. To the extent MSA is based on lack of factual support the motion is also DENIED. According to Defendant's July 20, 2017 letter Plaintiff's request for modification was denied, inter alia, due to all past due arrearages and fees assessed throughout the delinquency of the loan. There is a triable issue of fact whether Defendant's handling of the multiple modification requests caused (of partially caused) the basis for denial and contributed to the increase of the amounts owed diminishing Plaintiff's equity in the Property (if any). Hearing Required to address current status of CC 2923.6 (effective as of January 1, 2018). Parties to address Rankin v. Longs Drug Stores California, Inc. (2009) 169 Cal.App.4th 1246, 1256 ("When a pending action seeks recovery based on a statutorily-based obligation, and that statutory provision is repealed by legislation not containing an express saving clause, the California courts have consistently concluded the pending actions should be abated.") Hearing Required to address whether the first cause of action is moot an/or relief sought is duplicative. Specifically, (1) the determination of propriety of 2017 modification review will moot any failures associated with 2016 review; (2) the determination of impropriety of 2017 review will provide Plaintiff with injunctive relief until such time a proper review is conducted. Therefore, relief sought in the first cause of action based on earlier failure to comply with the law would be duplicative of the same relief associated with later failures. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: PACIFIC ARMS GOUGH STREET LLC VS. STEPHEN CAMPBELL ET AL (case is now regular civil case)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Requests For Documetns, Set One, Number 8 And 9; And A Statement Of Compliance Regarding Request For Documents Number 1; Request For Sanctions Against Plaintiff Pacific Arms Gough Street And Its Attorney William Lynn In The Amount Of $1,717
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 31, 2018, Line 5. DEFENDANT STEPHEN CAMPBELL'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Requests For Documetns, Set One, Number 8 And 9; And A Statement Of Compliance Regarding Request For Documents Number 1; Request For Sanctions Against Plaintiff Pacific Arms Gough Street And Its Attorney William Lynn In The Amount Of $1,717. GRANTED. Plaintiff shall supplement its production within 20 days of notice of entry of Order by producing responsive documents, including documents form and to Plaintiff's Property managers and other agent, as defined in the requests; and producing unreacted copies of the documents previously produced in redacted form. Parties to meet and confer on the terms of the protective order and present a proposed order to the Court at the time of the hearing. Plaintiff shall also provide a Code-compliant statement of compliance within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Defendant's request for sanctions is granted as requested in the amount of $1,717. =(501/CFH)
